channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Josh Shapiro barely escapes assassination; why Trump should call him
item.description: Political violence is out of hand – and Gov. Josh Shapiro, D-Penn., is the latest victim of it. It's imperative we take a very public, bipartisan stand against it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Joe Biden will speak about Social Security in his return to national stage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Joe Biden returns to the national stage Tuesday to elevate liberal concerns that President Donald Trump's agenda is threatening the health of Social Security. The 82-year-old Democrat has largely avoided speaking publicly since leaving the White House in January.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly believes Trump has several tariff deals already done
item.description: Bill O'Reilly recently spoke with President Trump and believes the president has several tariff deals already done.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he wants to imprison US citizens in El Salvador. That's likely illegal
item.description: President Donald Trump keeps talking about sending U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Army to control land on Mexico border as part of base, migrants could be detained, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A long sliver of federal land along the U.S.-Mexico border that President Donald Trump is turning over to the Department of Defense would be controlled by the Army as part of a base, which could allow troops to detain any trespassers, including migrants, U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harvard won’t comply with Trump admin’s demands amid threats of cutting federal funding
item.description: Harvard University President Alan M. Garber assured the community that the university would not comply with the Trump administration's threats to cut federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside California Politics: April 12, 2025
item.description: (INSIDE CALIFORNIA POLITICS) — This week on Inside California Politics, former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra sat down with host Nikki Laurenzo to discuss his campaign for California governor.
item.dc:creator: Inside California Politics, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know as Social Security anti-fraud measures take effect
item.description: Individuals who are flagged will be required to prove their identity in person for their Social Security claim to be processed.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect arsonist was prepared to attack Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro with hammer, court docs reveal
item.description: Court documents concerning Cody Balmer, the man charged in connection with a fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence, have revealed details of the suspect's alleged plot.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Both the US and El Salvador refuse to return wrongly deported man to the US
item.description: President Donald Trump’s top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they had no basis for the small Central American nation to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump trade war with China hurting US farmers, advocate says
item.description: John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation family farmer who works 1,300 acres in Virginia, said he hopes the Trump administration will listen to farmers moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to evacuate the building during the Jewish holiday of Passover.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence. It began gaining momentum after the Supreme Court’s 2022 ruling overturning Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Sisolak rematch with Lombardo? Former governor says he's considering it
item.description: Former Gov. Steve Sisolak is considering challenging Gov. Joe Lombardo in 2026, he told Politics Now host John Langeler on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal jobs watch: Just-released February jobs report shows few losses in Minnesota
item.description: The federal workforce includes nearly 34,000 jobs in Minnesota. Over 20,000 are located in the Twin Cities metropolitan area. The Bureau of Labor Statistics’ month jobs report shows a loss of 100 federal jobs through Minnesota from January to February.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Gawthrop and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five Black and Hispanic men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Rep. Ilhan Omar will run for reelection in the U.S. House, not an open Minnesota Senate seat
item.description: U.S. Rep. Ilhan Omar had been considering a run for the seat currently held by Sen. Tina Smith. The DFLer will instead seek a fifth House term.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: As measles spreads, federal budget cuts force closure of vaccine clinics
item.description: Federal funding cuts, though temporarily blocked by a judge, have upended vaccination outreach across the country, including in Arizona, Minnesota, Nevada, Texas, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith, Arielle Zionts, and Jackie Fortiér, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. stocks soar after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks surged on Wednesday after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on the higher tariffs he had imposed on most countries — except for China.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam, MPR News

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
